single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini's overlapping direction and lower edge (14% vs 16%), but range mismatch [0.4, 0.5] vs [0.5, 0.65] prevents consensus. Claude's high confidence and Iranian oil shock catalyst stronger than Gemini's medium confidence and supply chain concerns. Claude entry window closes 2026-04-29; Gemini 2026-04-13.

contradictions.explanation: Claude cites Atlanta Fed GDPNow at 1.3% after weak durable goods (high conviction, edge 18%). Gemini expects strong consumer spending and fiscal stimulus (medium conviction, edge 12%). Current implied_prob 0.705 and market price 0.7 align closer to Claude's NO thesis. Ranges non-overlapping ([0.45, 0.6] vs [0.8, 0.85]) indicates genuine disagreement on probability magnitude, not just depth. Atlanta Fed data point concrete; Gemini's fiscal stimulus expectations not grounded in live data source.

contradictions.market: Federal Reserve Rate Cut Before 2027 / No Fed Rate Cuts in 2026
contradictions.explanation: Claude ticker KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0 resolves end-2026; Gemini ticker KXRATECUT-26DEC31 resolves 2027-01-01. Scope mismatch clouds direct comparison. Claude prob_range [0.5, 0.65] midpoint 0.575 vs Gemini [0.4, 0.5] midpoint 0.45. Non-overlapping ranges. Both agree persistent inflation delays cuts, but Claude assigns higher probability to no cuts in 2026 specifically (implied by higher prob_range). Claude high confidence vs Gemini medium. Claude entry window longer (through 2026-04-29 vs 2026-04-13). Not consensus due to range non-overlap and ticker scope ambiguity.

portfolio_concentration_warning: Inflation cluster at weight 6 exceeds limit of 5. Recommend reducing CPI trade relative_weight to 5 or deferring GDP trade until directional clarity achieved. Both macro trades carry hawkish Fed / stagflation risk; diversification limited. No overlapping positions on Fed policy and inflation management reduces but does not eliminate correlation tail risk.
reeval_triggers: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update (Q1 GDP data drift >0.2% from 1.3%)
April CPI print (MoM > 0.5% breaks consensus trade thesis)
FOMC communications or Fed speaker hawkish/dovish pivot
Oil price shock or geopolitical escalation (Iran war expansion)
Labor market data (unemployment, payrolls) showing rapid softening
Market repricing of rate cut odds (CME FedWatch >30% probability)
